display.notes: Long range for a cancelable light normal, good whiff punish tool; 3f extra recovery on whiff; greatly extends hurtbox for 3f before active, making it susceptible to counterpokes; special/DR cancel is delayed until after active frames

raw.notes: Long range for a cancelable light normal, good whiff punish tool; 3f extra recovery on whiff; greatly extends hurtbox for 3f before active, making it susceptible to counterpokes; special/DR cancel is delayed until after active frames

display.notes: Good whiff punish tool when buffered into Target Combo or Super

raw.notes: Good whiff punish tool when buffered into Target Combo or Super

display.notes: Good whiff punish tool due to its long range and cancelability; extends a vertical collision box to reduce the risk of whiffing under opponent mid-juggle

raw.notes: Good whiff punish tool due to its long range and cancelability; extends a vertical collision box to reduce the risk of whiffing under opponent mid-juggle

display.notes: DR cancel is delayed until after 1st recovery frame

raw.notes: DR cancel is delayed until after 1st recovery frame

display.notes: Good neutral poke; 3f extra recovery on whiff; lifts up leg hurtbox on frames 4-11, making it strong vs. low attacks

raw.notes: Good neutral poke; 3f extra recovery on whiff; lifts up leg hurtbox on frames 4-11, making it strong vs. low attacks

display.notes: 8-15f Low Crush (feet are strike invincible); puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; Punish Counter launches opponent high into a limited juggle state; can be spaced for significantly better advantage; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles; first 2 active frames have shorter range (makes it easier to space properly); causes camera shake effect on block when connecting with final active frame (easier to tell when Luke has advantage)

raw.notes: 8-15f Low Crush (feet are strike invincible); puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; Punish Counter launches opponent high into a limited juggle state; can be spaced for significantly better advantage; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles; first 2 active frames have shorter range (makes it easier to space properly); causes camera shake effect on block when connecting with final active frame (easier to tell when Luke has advantage)

display.notes: Chains into 2LP/2LK; Luke's only 4f normal, useful in 3-hit light confirms; DR cancel is delayed until after 1st recovery frame; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-5 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

raw.notes: Chains into 2LP/2LK; Luke's only 4f normal, useful in 3-hit light confirms; DR cancel is delayed until after 1st recovery frame; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-5 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

display.notes: 3f extra recovery on whiff; great pressure tool with high reward on counter-hit or when canceled to Drive Rush; has an extended hurtbox on whiff until the end of recovery; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-7 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

raw.notes: 3f extra recovery on whiff; great pressure tool with high reward on counter-hit or when canceled to Drive Rush; has an extended hurtbox on whiff until the end of recovery; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-7 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

display.notes: Forces stand on hit; only the first active frame is cancelable; good anti-air hitbox but cannot hit cross-ups

raw.notes: Forces stand on hit; only the first active frame is cancelable; good anti-air hitbox but cannot hit cross-ups

display.notes: Chains into 2LP/2LK

raw.notes: Chains into 2LP/2LK

display.notes: Moves Luke forward, greatly increasing its range despite the stubby-looking animation; Range: 1.482 (1st active frame: 1.44); DR cancel is delayed until after active frames; has an extended hurtbox on whiff until the end of recovery

raw.notes: Moves Luke forward, greatly increasing its range despite the stubby-looking animation; Range: 1.482 (1st active frame: 1.44); DR cancel is delayed until after active frames; has an extended hurtbox on whiff until the end of recovery

display.notes: Has juggle potential; has a fixed 26f recovery on block regardless of which active frame connects (always -9 oB even when meaty)

raw.notes: Has juggle potential; has a fixed 26f recovery on block regardless of which active frame connects (always -9 oB even when meaty)

display.notes: Puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; has some juggle potential; can significantly delay the cancel into j.214P, allowing unique combos after pf.214[LP]; lower body projectile invuln 9-22f (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

raw.notes: Puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; has some juggle potential; can significantly delay the cancel into j.214P, allowing unique combos after pf.214[LP]; lower body projectile invuln 9-22f (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

display.notes: Spike knockdown on counter-hit/Punish Counter vs. airborne opponents; good close range jump-in and neutral jump throw bait tool; lower body projectile invuln 9-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

raw.notes: Spike knockdown on counter-hit/Punish Counter vs. airborne opponents; good close range jump-in and neutral jump throw bait tool; lower body projectile invuln 9-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

display.notes: Cross-up

raw.notes: Cross-up

display.notes: Cross-up; lower body projectile invuln 7-20f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

raw.notes: Cross-up; lower body projectile invuln 7-20f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

display.notes: Better horizontal range than j.HP for long-range jumps; lower body projectile invuln 10-19f except around attack hitbox, and 10-24f for lowest part of foot hurtbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

raw.notes: Better horizontal range than j.HP for long-range jumps; lower body projectile invuln 10-19f except around attack hitbox, and 10-24f for lowest part of foot hurtbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

display.notes: Causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; has some juggle potential

raw.notes: Causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; has some juggle potential

display.notes: Pulls back hurtbox frames 5-12 for an auto-shimmy tool (also very strong when used out of Drive Rush)

raw.notes: Pulls back hurtbox frames 5-12 for an auto-shimmy tool (also very strong when used out of Drive Rush)

display.notes: Whiffs on crouching opponents at farther ranges unless they extend a vertical hurtbox; puts airborne opponents into spinning limited juggle state; no head hurtbox during active frames, making it a useful anti-air; after DR~4HK on block, Luke is at the edge of throw range and can also threaten with 2LK~2LP to punish walk back, or 2LP to beat reversal 4f normals; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles

raw.notes: Whiffs on crouching opponents at farther ranges unless they extend a vertical hurtbox; puts airborne opponents into spinning limited juggle state; no head hurtbox during active frames, making it a useful anti-air; after DR~4HK on block, Luke is at the edge of throw range and can also threaten with 2LK~2LP to punish walk back, or 2LP to beat reversal 4f normals; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles

display.notes: 4f extra recovery on whiff; 5f gap on block into TC follow-up

raw.notes: 4f extra recovery on whiff; 5f gap on block into TC follow-up

display.notes: () refers to scaled damage when starting combo with 5LP

raw.notes: () refers to scaled damage when starting combo with 5LP

display.notes: () refers to scaled damage when starting combo with 5LP; puts opponent into limited juggle state; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd active frame

raw.notes: () refers to scaled damage when starting combo with 5LP; puts opponent into limited juggle state; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd active frame

display.notes: Useful buffer from 5MP in neutral that can end with Super or the full Target Combo sequence; Super cancel is delayed until after 4th recovery frame

raw.notes: Useful buffer from 5MP in neutral that can end with Super or the full Target Combo sequence; Super cancel is delayed until after 4th recovery frame

display.notes: () refers to scaled damage

raw.notes: () refers to scaled damage

display.notes: () refers to scaled damage; full sequence builds significant Drive gauge; knockdown grants a perfect meaty Dash + 5HK (+1 on Block even vs. midscreen Back Rise)

raw.notes: () refers to scaled damage; full sequence builds significant Drive gauge; knockdown grants a perfect meaty Dash + 5HK (+1 on Block even vs. midscreen Back Rise)

display.notes: 5f gap on block between hits; causes a staggered tailspin when performed after Punish Counter 6HP (allows a cancel into fully charged Flash Knuckle of any strength or a +28 DR Cancel); this Punish Counter version forces stand and does not incur any extra damage scaling

raw.notes: 5f gap on block between hits; causes a staggered tailspin when performed after Punish Counter 6HP (allows a cancel into fully charged Flash Knuckle of any strength or a +28 DR Cancel); this Punish Counter version forces stand and does not incur any extra damage scaling

display.notes: Has access to corner throw loop, requires walking with manual timing (4f timing leniency to beat reversal 4f buttons); a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

raw.notes: Has access to corner throw loop, requires walking with manual timing (4f timing leniency to beat reversal 4f buttons); a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

display.notes: Side switches; no throw oki after back throwing opponent into corner, but can threaten with meaty strikes; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

raw.notes: Side switches; no throw oki after back throwing opponent into corner, but can threaten with meaty strikes; a successful Throw Escape builds 0.5 Drive bars for the defender; Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

display.notes: See Drive Impact. Airborne connect gives spinning juggle state on Punish Counter or corner Wall Splat with variable height.

raw.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Impact|Drive Impact]]. Airborne connect gives spinning juggle state on Punish Counter or corner Wall Splat with variable height.

display.notes: Performed by inputting 6HPHK during blockstun; 5f extra recovery on hit; freezes the screen for 4f during startup; see Drive Reversal.

raw.notes: Performed by inputting 6HPHK during blockstun; 5f extra recovery on hit; freezes the screen for 4f during startup; see [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Reversal|Drive Reversal]].

display.notes: Performed by holding 6HPHK on wakeup; 5f extra recovery on hit; does not have any screen freeze except for a 12f freeze when interacting with the opponent's throw hitbox (this also refunds 1 Drive bar after punishing the throw); see Drive Reversal.

raw.notes: Performed by holding 6HPHK on wakeup; 5f extra recovery on hit; does not have any screen freeze except for a 12f freeze when interacting with the opponent's throw hitbox (this also refunds 1 Drive bar after punishing the throw); see [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Reversal|Drive Reversal]].

display.notes: See Drive Parry. Perfect Parrying has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack. Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 11 frames of recovery. After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.

raw.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Parry|Drive Parry]]. Perfect Parrying has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack. Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 11 frames of recovery. After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.

display.notes: See Drive Rush; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.817 (min/throw), 2.499 (min/block), 3.461 (max/final DR frame)

raw.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Rush|Drive Rush]]; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.817 (min/throw), 2.499 (min/block), 3.461 (max/final DR frame)

display.notes: See Drive Rush; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties

raw.notes: See [[Street_Fighter_6/Gauges#Drive_Rush|Drive Rush]]; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties

raw.notes: 1-hit OD projectile; costs 1 extra Drive bar; () refers to scaled damage after 236PP connects; causes a wallsplat on hit (can combo near the corner, or whiff 236K~P to safely gain ground); extends a projectile invuln hurtbox on frames 10-32 (more vulnerable to ranged jumps and anti-projectile moves)

display.notes: Reduced damage and higher juggle start/increase/limit on active frames 3-10; cannot hit cross-up; does not juggle into SA3 despite being cancelable; much higher juggle limit than other versions of Rising Uppercut (useful in corner juggles)

display.notes: Reduced damage 3rd active frame of 1st hit (will only do 1 hit for 600 damage on later active frames); cannot hit cross-up; can cancel into PP follow-up on hit for 1 Drive bar; 2nd hit whiffs vs. crouch block (reduced chip damage, worse advantage)

raw.notes: Reduced damage 3rd active frame of 1st hit (will only do 1 hit for 600 damage on later active frames); cannot hit cross-up; can cancel into PP follow-up on hit for 1 Drive bar; 2nd hit whiffs vs. crouch block (reduced chip damage, worse advantage)

display.notes: Follow-up on hit only for 1 additional Drive bar; can whiff against high juggled opponent; counts as a separate hit for damage scaling

raw.notes: Follow-up on hit only for 1 additional Drive bar; can whiff against high juggled opponent; counts as a separate hit for damage scaling

display.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state; no head hurtbox on frames 6-17 for improved anti-air; the final 2 recovery frames are cancelable on Hit into specials/Supers (enables a corner juggle into 623LP or SA2)

raw.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state; no head hurtbox on frames 6-17 for improved anti-air; the final 2 recovery frames are cancelable on Hit into specials/Supers (enables a corner juggle into 623LP or SA2)

display.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state; active frames 3-4 can nullify normal 1-hit projectiles; canceling to SA3 will cause juggle to whiff; 1f more recovery on hit/block; no head hurtbox on frames 6-29 for improved anti-air

raw.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state; active frames 3-4 can nullify normal 1-hit projectiles; canceling to SA3 will cause juggle to whiff; 1f more recovery on hit/block; no head hurtbox on frames 6-29 for improved anti-air

display.notes: Hold LP 18-20f for perfect version; launches opponent high into a limited juggle state; active frames 3-4 can nullify normal 1-hit projectiles; canceling to SA3 will cause juggle to whiff; 1f more recovery on hit/block; no head hurtbox on frames 6-29 for improved anti-air

display.notes: Wall Bounce on hit for a limited juggle state; active frames 3-4 can nullify normal 1-hit projectiles; higher KD Adv when farther from wall (opponent may bounce over Luke's head if too close to the wall); SA3 cancel only juggles properly in corner; 1f more recovery on hit/block; cannot combo naturally when canceled from 2HP

display.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state (can only juggle into canceled SA3); worse KD advantage vs. airborne opponents (+31 when juggled after 5LP~MP~HP); can cancel into SA3 on reaction if opponent attempts a Drive Impact; pulls back hurtbox on frames 5-15

raw.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state (can only juggle into canceled SA3); worse KD advantage vs. airborne opponents (+31 when juggled after 5LP~MP~HP); can cancel into SA3 on reaction if opponent attempts a Drive Impact; pulls back hurtbox on frames 5-15

display.notes: 1f extra recovery on hit/block; active frames 3-4 can nullify normal 1-hit projectiles; cancelable into SA3 on reaction if opponent attempts a Drive Impact; pulls back hurtbox on frames 5-25; leaves opponent close on block for a 2LK~2LP hitconfirm or a 1f microwalk into throw (slightly longer if opponent walks back)

raw.notes: 1f extra recovery on hit/block; active frames 3-4 can nullify normal 1-hit projectiles; cancelable into SA3 on reaction if opponent attempts a Drive Impact; pulls back hurtbox on frames 5-25; leaves opponent close on block for a 2LK~2LP hitconfirm or a 1f microwalk into throw (slightly longer if opponent walks back)

display.notes: Hold HP 18-20f for perfect version; active frames 3-4 can nullify normal 1-hit projectiles; 1f more recovery on hit/block; cancelable into SA3 on reaction if opponent attempts a Drive Impact; pulls back hurtbox on frames 5-25; leaves opponent close on block for a 2LK~2LP hitconfirm or a 1f microwalk into throw (slightly longer if opponent walks back)

raw.notes: Hold HP 18-20f for perfect version; active frames 3-4 can nullify normal 1-hit projectiles; 1f more recovery on hit/block; cancelable into SA3 on reaction if opponent attempts a Drive Impact; pulls back hurtbox on frames 5-25; leaves opponent close on block for a 2LK~2LP hitconfirm or a 1f microwalk into throw (slightly longer if opponent walks back)

display.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state; active frames 3-4 can nullify normal 1-hit projectiles; listed recovery is for blocked/whiffed version (on hit, automatically transitions to 2nd hit); 1st hit is cancelable into PP follow-up for 1 extra Drive bar on hit only; can juggle directly into any Super (canceling into Super will cause SA3 to whiff; SA2 will whiff on final cancelable frame)

raw.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state; active frames 3-4 can nullify normal 1-hit projectiles; listed recovery is for blocked/whiffed version (on hit, automatically transitions to 2nd hit); 1st hit is cancelable into PP follow-up for 1 extra Drive bar on hit only; can juggle directly into any Super (canceling into Super will cause SA3 to whiff; SA2 will whiff on final cancelable frame)

display.notes: Costs 1 extra Drive bar on 214PP 1st hit only (cannot activate on block or when armor absorbed); leaves Luke close enough to walk in for a strike/throw mixup; counts as a separate hit from 214PP for damage scaling; in juggles, only connects on opponents low to the ground and will not come out if opponent is juggled higher

raw.notes: Costs 1 extra Drive bar on 214PP 1st hit only (cannot activate on block or when armor absorbed); leaves Luke close enough to walk in for a strike/throw mixup; counts as a separate hit from 214PP for damage scaling; in juggles, only connects on opponents low to the ground and will not come out if opponent is juggled higher

display.notes: Can be input during forward/neutral jump (or back jump when canceled from j.MP); 7f extra landing recovery on whiff if input during the first 32 jump frames; Luke is in a crouching state from 4th landing recovery frame onward; improved frame advantage when done lower to the ground, and spaced out at farther ranges; can only be up to -1 on block if not precisely spaced; can partially charge the attack and release early for better juggle advantage after 214[LP]

raw.notes: Can be input during forward/neutral jump (or back jump when canceled from j.MP); 7f extra landing recovery on whiff if input during the first 32 jump frames; Luke is in a crouching state from 4th landing recovery frame onward; improved frame advantage when done lower to the ground, and spaced out at farther ranges; can only be up to -1 on block if not precisely spaced; can partially charge the attack and release early for better juggle advantage after 214[LP]

display.notes: Can be input during forward/neutral jump (or back jump when canceled from j.MP); the final 12 recovery frames are in a crouching state; puts the opponent into a limited juggle state (can follow-up with 623LP if opponent is juggled at max height)

raw.notes: Can be input during forward/neutral jump (or back jump when canceled from j.MP); the final 12 recovery frames are in a crouching state; puts the opponent into a limited juggle state (can follow-up with 623LP if opponent is juggled at max height)

display.notes: Can be input during forward/neutral jump (or back jump when canceled from j.MP); the final 12 recovery frames are in a crouching state; puts the opponent into a limited juggle state; kara-canceling the startup with j.214P~PP can affect Luke's trajectory and frame advantage

raw.notes: Can be input during forward/neutral jump (or back jump when canceled from j.MP); the final 12 recovery frames are in a crouching state; puts the opponent into a limited juggle state; kara-canceling the startup with j.214P~PP can affect Luke's trajectory and frame advantage

display.notes: Follow-up P or K can be input on frames 12-32; Luke is in a counter-hit state for entire duration

raw.notes: Follow-up P or K can be input on frames 12-32; Luke is in a counter-hit state for entire duration

display.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state; very late connect to juggle into 623LP/MP, SA1, or SA2 (otherwise, can only cancel into SA3)

raw.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state; very late connect to juggle into 623LP/MP, SA1, or SA2 (otherwise, can only cancel into SA3)

display.notes: First 2 active frames can only hit airborne opponents; 3rd active frame hits grounded and gives the best KD Advantage (cannot hit meaty to allow a follow-up juggle); Luke is in a crouching state for entire recovery duration; puts opponents into limited juggle state; 5f extra recovery on whiff

raw.notes: First 2 active frames can only hit airborne opponents; 3rd active frame hits grounded and gives the best KD Advantage (cannot hit meaty to allow a follow-up juggle); Luke is in a crouching state for entire recovery duration; puts opponents into limited juggle state; 5f extra recovery on whiff

display.notes: Armor does not apply to follow-up attacks; follow-up P or K can be input up to frame 32; follow-up can start immediately after armor hit freeze without waiting for any additional Avenger startup frames to occur; Luke is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration

raw.notes: Armor does not apply to follow-up attacks; follow-up P or K can be input up to frame 32; follow-up can start immediately after armor hit freeze without waiting for any additional Avenger startup frames to occur; Luke is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration

display.notes: Puts opponent into limited juggle state; very late connect to juggle into 623LP/MP, SA1, or SA2 (otherwise, can only cancel into SA2 or SA3); midscreen, later active frames canceled into SA2 will whiff

display.notes: First 2 active frames can only hit airborne opponents; Luke is in a crouching state for entire recovery duration; puts opponent into limited juggle state

raw.notes: First 2 active frames can only hit airborne opponents; Luke is in a crouching state for entire recovery duration; puts opponent into limited juggle state

display.notes: 5 hit Super projectile, first 4 hits travel 65% screen, final hit goes fullscreen; higher KD Adv. when farther from wall; extends a projectile-vulnerable hurtbox during startup frames 2-5 (more difficult to use as an anti-fireball tool)

raw.notes: 5 hit Super projectile, first 4 hits travel 65% screen, final hit goes fullscreen; higher KD Adv. when farther from wall; extends a projectile-vulnerable hurtbox during startup frames 2-5 (more difficult to use as an anti-fireball tool)

display.notes: Extremely fast super that works well in juggles; in the corner, gives Luke an auto-timed strike/throw mixup; on hit, the animation is not invincible until the very end (can be hit by delayed projectiles like Kimberly Spraycan or JP Amnesia orbs); Full Dmg distribution: 300x3,200x6,700

raw.notes: Extremely fast super that works well in juggles; in the corner, gives Luke an auto-timed strike/throw mixup; on hit, the animation is not invincible until the very end (can be hit by delayed projectiles like Kimberly Spraycan or JP Amnesia orbs); Full Dmg distribution: 300x3,200x6,700

display.notes: Luke runs forward; active frames occur upon reaching the opponent or on frame 27 (whichever occurs first) but invincibility is not extended when far from opponent; has juggle potential; on block, bounces away with a fixed recovery period (whiff recovery is 26f slower); cinematic time regenerates ~2 Drive bars for Luke; takes opponent to Memphis

raw.notes: Luke runs forward; active frames occur upon reaching the opponent or on frame 27 (whichever occurs first) but invincibility is not extended when far from opponent; has juggle potential; on block, bounces away with a fixed recovery period (whiff recovery is 26f slower); cinematic time regenerates ~2 Drive bars for Luke; takes opponent to Memphis

display.notes: Luke runs forward; active frames occur upon reaching the opponent or on frame 27 (whichever occurs first) but invincibility is not extended when far from opponent; has juggle potential; on block, bounces away with a fixed recovery period (whiff recovery is 26f slower); cinematic time regenerates ~2.3 Drive bars for Luke; available at 25% HP or below; does not take opponent to Memphis

raw.notes: Luke runs forward; active frames occur upon reaching the opponent or on frame 27 (whichever occurs first) but invincibility is not extended when far from opponent; has juggle potential; on block, bounces away with a fixed recovery period (whiff recovery is 26f slower); cinematic time regenerates ~2.3 Drive bars for Luke; available at 25% HP or below; does not take opponent to Memphis
